body.title: CLOSED: Fix update-usage.sh hook output generation
body.result: Fixed shell-to-Python variable passing: export USAGE_RESPONSE/PLAN_TYPE/CACHE_FILE, read via os.environ in heredoc. Heredoc delimiter quoted to prevent shell expansion inside Python.
body.description:
The update-usage.sh hook (Claude Code stop hook for KDE Plasma widget) fails to generate required cache output due to improper shell variable interpolation in the Python heredoc. Variables $USAGE_RESPONSE and $PLAN_TYPE are not properly escaped before passing to Python json.loads() and dict construction, causing silent failures when response contains special chars or when plan type is empty.

**Affected files:** /home/akien/.claude/hooks/update-usage.sh
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Fix the shell-to-Python variable passing mechanism; ensure cache file is properly generated on every successful API call. Out of scope: API endpoint changes, credential file format changes.
**Test plan:** Run hook after Claude Code session (or manually invoke with mocked credentials); verify usage-cache.json is created with correct structure (usage object, planType string, updatedAt timestamp). Test with response containing special chars (quotes, newlines).
**Pass condition:** Hook generates valid usage-cache.json file after Claude Code stop with correct structure (usage, planType, updatedAt fields and timestamp).
